gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: The study aims to develop a versatile method for generating and screening smart enzyme libraries to engineer Candida antarctica lipase A (Cal-A) for altered substrate selectivity, specifically discrimination between short-chain and long-chain fatty acid triglycerides. Traditional methods suffer from high cost, experimental difficulty, time-consuming steps, and lack of flexibility in recombining mutations. The Golden Gate strategy using type IIS restriction enzymes is proposed to enable seamless, unidirectional assembly of independently mutated gene fragments, combined with an automated whole-cell screening assay.

section_gists.heading: Abstract
section_gists.gist: The Golden Gate strategy uses type IIS restriction enzymes for seamless recombination of DNA fragments. Hot-spots for mutations were identified in three regions of Cal-A. Gene segments were mutated using targeted or random methods, then reassembled to create diverse libraries. An automated screening method was developed to visually discriminate hydrolysis of short-chain vs long-chain fatty acid substrates by whole cells.
process_signals: Hot-spots individuated in three distinct regions of Cal-A
Three gene segments subjected to most appropriate mutagenesis method
Straightforward reassembly allowed combining products of different mutagenesis methods in a single round
Screening aided by development of a general, automated method for visual discrimination
methods: Golden Gate assembly with type IIS restriction enzymes
Targeted and random mutagenesis of gene segments

section_gists.heading: Introduction
section_gists.gist: Effective mutagenesis requires small, targeted, high-quality 'smart' libraries due to screening constraints. Simultaneous mutations can have non-additive effects. Existing methods have disadvantages: high cost, experimental demand, time, and inflexibility in recombining mutations. Golden Gate assembly is proposed to overcome these by allowing independent mutagenesis of gene segments and seamless recombination. Cal-A is chosen as a model enzyme due to its unique properties and industrial potential for discriminating short-chain vs long-chain fatty acids. The substrate binding mode is undefined, so a smart library targeting the putative binding area is needed.
process_signals: Need for creative solutions to explore combinatorial complexity
Existing methods suffer from disadvantages: high cost, experimentally demanding, time-consuming, lack flexibility
Golden Gate strategy inspired by synthetic biology applications
Cal-A chosen for its unique properties and industrial relevance
Hypothesis: targeting tunnel region and key residues may modulate substrate specificity
methods: Smart library design combining targeted and random mutagenesis
failures_or_limits: Existing methods: high cost, experimental demand, time, inflexibility
Substrate binding mode of Cal-A remains undefined

section_gists.heading: Library design
section_gists.gist: Smart library design based on structural analysis: tunnel region (residues 211-350) targeted for random mutagenesis; residues Tyr93, Tyr183, Phe431 selected for NDT saturation mutagenesis. The gene was divided into three parts for independent mutagenesis and reassembled using Golden Gate with BsaI and SapI. This allows combining random and focused mutagenesis, exploring synergistic effects. Ten libraries were generated with various combinations of mutated and native parts. The method enables easy recombination and flexibility.

Tyr93, Tyr183, Phe431 selected based on proximity to catalytic triad and structural analysis
NDT degeneracy chosen for good chemical diversity with modest library size
Gene divided into three parts for independent mutagenesis
Golden Gate assembly with BsaI and SapI for seamless recombination
Ten libraries generated with different combinations
methods: Golden Gate assembly with BsaI and SapI

section_gists.heading: Screening
section_gists.gist: An automated screening assay was developed using a liquid-handler robot to inoculate saturated cultures onto agar plates with emulsified triglycerides (tributyrin for C4, olive oil/rhodamine for C18). Active clones produce halos (clear or fluorescent). The method is qualitative but robust and reproducible. Screening of library 10 revealed variants with altered selectivity: some preferentially hydrolyzed olive oil, others tributyrin, while most showed no improved selectivity or lost activity. The wild-type was active on both substrates without distinction.

section_gists.heading: Conclusions
section_gists.gist: The method offers maximum versatility in generating and screening smart libraries. Golden Gate enables quick library combination and separate treatment of gene parts with different mutagenesis strategies. It allows exploring epistasis and does not require engineering restriction sites. The method was successfully applied to alter Cal-A selectivity as a proof of concept. It can be extended to more than three parts.

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: The research aimed to develop a flexible, efficient method for generating smart enzyme libraries by combining Golden Gate assembly with automated screening.
The hypothesis was that independent mutagenesis of gene segments and seamless recombination would allow exploration of synergistic mutations and improve substrate selectivity.
Cal-A was chosen as a model due to its industrial relevance and undefined substrate binding, making it a good test case for the method.
The study likely progressed from method development to proof-of-concept demonstration, with screening revealing variants with altered selectivity, validating the approach.

initial_request.research_problem: Develop a versatile method for generating and screening smart enzyme libraries to engineer Candida antarctica lipase A (Cal-A) for altered substrate selectivity between short-chain and long-chain fatty acid triglycerides.
initial_request.known_context: Existing smart library methods suffer from high cost, experimental demand, time consumption, and lack of flexibility in recombining mutations.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Existing smart library methods are costly, experimentally demanding, time-consuming, and inflexible for recombining mutations.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need for a flexible, cost-effective method to generate and screen smart libraries for enzyme engineering.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A synthetic biology approach using Golden Gate assembly can enable rapid generation of recombined functional diversity in Cal-A.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Divide Cal-A gene into three parts for independent mutagenesis and design a Golden Gate assembly strategy.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Gene divided into part 1 (N-terminal, residues 11-210), part 2 (tunnel region, 211-350), part 3 (C-terminal, 351-end).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design mutagenesis strategies for each part.

evidence.quote_or_span: These methods typically suffer from one or more disadvantage including high cost, being experimentally demanding, requiring time consuming steps or, importantly, not providing flexibility in recombining mutations.
The three ‘parts’ of Cal-A were synthesized in a codon-optimized form
the entire tunnel region (residues 211-350) was targeted for random mutagenesis
By sequencing approximately 50 clones from each library, we identified 11 of the 12 codons expected for the Tyr93 libraries, 10 of 12 for the Tyr183 libraries and 9 of 12 for the Phe431 libraries.
PART 1 (N-terminal region 11–210, in green) is comprised of the $\alpha/\beta$ fold and includes Tyr93 and Tyr183.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Cal-A gene divided into three parts.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which mutagenesis strategies to apply to each part to explore substrate selectivity.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Targeted NDT saturation mutagenesis at Tyr93, Tyr183, Phe431 and random mutagenesis of the tunnel region will generate functional diversity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Apply NDT saturation mutagenesis to Tyr93, Tyr183, Phe431; error-prone PCR to tunnel region (part 2).
evolution_trajectory.observation: NDT degeneracy chosen for three positions; error-prone PCR for tunnel region.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design assembly method to recombine mutated parts.

evidence.quote_or_span: We chose NDT degeneracy at these three positions
the entire tunnel region (residues 211-350) was targeted for random mutagenesis

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a seamless method to recombine mutated parts into expression vector.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Golden Gate assembly with BsaI and SapI will allow seamless recombination of parts.

evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Generate libraries using the designed assembly.

evidence.quote_or_span: The junctions between parts were designed with a unique BsaI restriction site while two unique SapI restriction sites were designed to ligate the assembled gene into the daughter vector

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether the assembly strategy can generate diverse libraries.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Golden Gate assembly will yield at least 10^3 transformants per library with various combinations.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Generate ten libraries with combinations of mutated and native parts.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Ten libraries obtained with at least 10^3 transformants each; one library (Tyr93-Phe431) initially failed with circularized plasmid.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Assess library quality and resolve failure.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Libraries generated with one failure resolved.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether libraries have expected diversity and quality.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Sequencing will confirm expected diversity for NDT libraries and mutational load for random library.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Sequence pooled clones from NDT libraries and 20 clones from random library.
evolution_trajectory.observation: NDT libraries showed expected codon degeneracy; random library had total mutational load 34, 25% wild-type, average 1.65 mutations/clone, max 4.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed to screening of libraries.

evidence.quote_or_span: The quality of each of the degenerate NDT libraries, whether resulting from mutation of one part or assembly of more than one mutated part, was routinely assessed by sequencing pooled clones.
codon degeneracy can be clearly visible in the DNA sequencing electropherogram (Fig 3), confirming the expected distribution of nucleotides instead of the original codon.
The quality of the randomly mutated library was assessed by sequencing 20 clones (Table 2).

The 20 clones carried a total mutational load of 34, with approximately 25% of the clones being wild-type and the majority of clones carrying two mutations (average = 1.65), for a maximum of 4 mutations per clone.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether variants show altered substrate selectivity between short-chain (C4) and long-chain (C18) triglycerides.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Automated whole-cell screening against tributyrin and olive oil/rhodamine will identify variants with altered selectivity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen 735 clones from Cal-A libraries using liquid handler robot for halo-based assays.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Screening performed; wild-type active on both substrates without distinction; 88 clones (12%) showed discrimination.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze screening results to identify selectivity patterns.

evidence.quote_or_span: The resulting Cal-A libraries expressed in E. coli BL21 (DE3) were screened to evaluate selectivity of hydrolase activity toward long versus short-chain triglycerides.
For the olive/oil rhodamine plates, the tributyrin was replaced with 2.5 g olive oil, and rhodamine was added at a concentration of 0.001% w/v.
A script was designed to pick up 20 μL of culture, column by column, from the inoculum plates and spot 8 μL of inoculum by lightly touching the agar surface.
A total of 735 clones were screened (Table 1).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which variants show altered selectivity and what are the mutation effects.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Variants with mutations at specific positions will show distinct selectivity profiles.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze halo patterns to classify variants as long-chain preferring, short-chain preferring, or non-discriminating.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Variant B6 prefers olive oil; D4, D6, G9 prefer tributyrin; majority show no improved selectivity or lose activity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Correlate mutations with activity and selectivity.

evidence.quote_or_span: variant B6 hydrolyzes olive oil preferentially over tributyrin, while variants D4, D6 and G9 show the inverse selectivity.
the wild-type was active with tributyrin and olive oil without distinction.

The majority of the variants do not show any improved selectivity compared to the wild-type Cal-A (e.g. A2, A3, the majority of row B, etc, relative to wild-type A11, starred) or they lose the ability to hydrolyze both of the substrates (A1, A4, A5, and so on).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How mutations at Tyr93, Tyr183, Phe431 affect activity and selectivity.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Tyr93 mutation retains activity with long-chain preference; Tyr183 is crucial for activity; Phe431 tolerates mutation without selectivity change.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Calculate activity retention and selectivity preference for each position.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Tyr93: 89% active, long-chain preference; Tyr183: 10% active, short-chain preference; Phe431: 90% active, no discrimination.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate assembly method and note limitations.

evidence.quote_or_span: When Tyr93 was mutated, 89% of the clones retained activity and discriminated preferentially towards long-chain fatty acids.
In contrast, Tyr183 was crucial for activity: when mutated, only 10% of the variants retained activity, with a preference for the hydrolysis of short-chain fatty acids.
Phe431 tolerated mutation without little effect, as activity was maintained in 90% of the variants and no increased discrimination was found.

evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Note limitations of screening method.

evidence.quote_or_span: These results demonstrate the suitability of the Golden Gate strategy to achieve rapid generation of readily recombined functional diversity.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Limitations of the screening assay.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Halo analysis is qualitative and not quantitative due to variability in colony size.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge that the method is qualitatively robust but not quantitative.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Halo analysis gives yes/no activity response; robust qualitatively but not quantitative due to cell number variability.

evidence.quote_or_span: Halo analysis is qualitative, giving a yes/no response as to activity of a variant toward a specific substrate.

findings.problem: Missing required mainline events K11 (one-pot assembly failure) and K12 (colony PCR validation). These events are part of the canonical trajectory and must be covered.

findings.problem: Step 1 covers too many events (K01, K02, K03, K04, K14) in a single step, making it a generic summary rather than agent-like decisions. It should be split into distinct steps for hypothesis, design, and validation.
findings.revision_instruction: Split step 1 into separate steps: one for problem identification (K01), one for gene division (K02), one for mutagenesis strategy (K03), and one for Golden Gate design (K04). Move K14 coverage to a later validation step.

findings.problem: Step 6 covers K08, K09, and K10, mixing screening execution with analysis of results. The numbers '88 clones (12%)' are not directly evidenced in the step's evidence list.
findings.revision_instruction: Separate screening execution (K08) from analysis of selectivity (K09) and mutation effects (K10). Ensure each step has direct evidence for any numbers mentioned.

findings.problem: Step 1 includes evidence EV_AUTO_002 about sequencing 50 clones, which is not directly related to the gene division decision and belongs to library quality assessment (K07).
findings.revision_instruction: Remove EV_AUTO_002 from step 1 and place it in the step covering library quality assessment (K07).

findings.problem: Step 1 mentions residue numbers '11-210' and '351-end' which are not directly evidenced in the provided evidence quotes.
findings.revision_instruction: Either add evidence that explicitly states these residue ranges or remove the specific numbers if not critical.

self_critic.summary: The candidate trajectory is missing two required mainline events (K11 and K12) and has steps that are overly broad, covering multiple canonical events in a single step. Step 1 is a generic summary rather than a sequence of agent-like decisions. Step 6 conflates screening execution with analysis. Some evidence is misplaced, and specific numbers lack direct evidence. Revision should add missing events, split broad steps, and ensure evidence alignment.

evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen 735 clones from Cal-A libraries using liquid handler robot for halo-based assays.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Screening performed; wild-type active on both substrates without distinction.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze screening results to identify selectivity patterns.

evidence.quote_or_span: The resulting Cal-A libraries expressed in E. coli BL21 (DE3) were screened to evaluate selectivity of hydrolase activity toward long versus short-chain triglycerides.
For the olive/oil rhodamine plates, the tributyrin was replaced with 2.5 g olive oil, and rhodamine was added at a concentration of 0.001% w/v.
A script was designed to pick up 20 μL of culture, column by column, from the inoculum plates and spot 8 μL of inoculum by lightly touching the agar surface.
A total of 735 clones were screened (Table 1).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which variants show altered selectivity and what are the mutation effects.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Variants with mutations at specific positions will show distinct selectivity profiles.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze halo patterns to classify variants as long-chain preferring, short-chain preferring, or non-discriminating.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Variant B6 prefers olive oil; D4, D6, G9 prefer tributyrin; majority show no improved selectivity or lose activity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Correlate mutations with activity and selectivity.

evidence.quote_or_span: variant B6 hydrolyzes olive oil preferentially over tributyrin, while variants D4, D6 and G9 show the inverse selectivity.
the wild-type was active with tributyrin and olive oil without distinction.

The majority of the variants do not show any improved selectivity compared to the wild-type Cal-A (e.g. A2, A3, the majority of row B, etc, relative to wild-type A11, starred) or they lose the ability to hydrolyze both of the substrates (A1, A4, A5, and so on).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How mutations at Tyr93, Tyr183, Phe431 affect activity and selectivity.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Tyr93 mutation retains activity with long-chain preference; Tyr183 is crucial for activity; Phe431 tolerates mutation without selectivity change.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Calculate activity retention and selectivity preference for each position.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Tyr93: 89% active, long-chain preference; Tyr183: 10% active, short-chain preference; Phe431: 90% active, no discrimination.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate assembly method and note limitations.

evidence.quote_or_span: When Tyr93 was mutated, 89% of the clones retained activity and discriminated preferentially towards long-chain fatty acids.
In contrast, Tyr183 was crucial for activity: when mutated, only 10% of the variants retained activity, with a preference for the hydrolysis of short-chain fatty acids.
Phe431 tolerated mutation without little effect, as activity was maintained in 90% of the variants and no increased discrimination was found.

evolution_trajectory.decision: Test simplified one-pot assembly for recombining mutant parts.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Simplified one-pot assembly had reduced success rate for mutant parts; standard method was used instead.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate standard method with colony PCR and sequencing.

evidence.quote_or_span: when we applied this simplified strategy to achieve the recombination of the mutant parts, a reduced success rate of ligation was achieved.
the use of our standard method (Fig 2 in main article) solved the problem.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Standard method used after one-pot failure.

evolution_trajectory.observation: 100% correct ligation products observed for recombined libraries.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed to overall validation of the Golden Gate strategy.

evidence.quote_or_span: Upon screening more than 15 randomly-selected clones for each recombined library, we observed 100% correct ligation products by colony PCR.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Mutation effects analyzed and assembly validated.

evolution_trajectory.observation: Results demonstrate suitability of Golden Gate strategy for rapid generation of recombined functional diversity.

evidence.quote_or_span: These results demonstrate the suitability of the Golden Gate strategy to achieve rapid generation of readily recombined functional diversity.

evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Halo analysis is qualitative and not quantitative due to variability in colony size.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge that the method is qualitatively robust but not quantitative.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Halo analysis gives yes/no activity response; robust qualitatively but not quantitative due to cell number variability.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Complete trajectory.

evidence.quote_or_span: Halo analysis is qualitative, giving a yes/no response as to activity of a variant toward a specific substrate.
The method is qualitatively robust, in that an active variant consistently shows a halo and a negative variant consistently does not

the current method does not lend itself to quantitative activity measurements because of factors including insufficient precision in the number of cells inoculated which affects colony size and thus appearance of the halo

validation_methods: Colony PCR and sequencing of recombined libraries
success_verification.final_conclusion: The Golden Gate strategy successfully generated recombined functional diversity, enabling identification of Cal-A variants with altered substrate selectivity, though the screening method is qualitative.

source.title: Enzyme engineering: A synthetic biology approach for more effective library generation and automated high-throughput screening.

initial_request.research_problem: Develop a versatile method for generating and screening smart enzyme libraries to engineer Candida antarctica lipase A (Cal-A) for altered substrate selectivity between short-chain and long-chain fatty acid triglycerides.
initial_request.known_context: Existing smart library methods suffer from high cost, experimental demand, time consumption, and lack of flexibility in recombining mutations.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Existing smart library methods are costly, experimentally demanding, time-consuming, and inflexible for recombining mutations.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need for a flexible, cost-effective method to generate and screen smart libraries for enzyme engineering.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A synthetic biology approach using Golden Gate assembly can enable rapid generation of recombined functional diversity in Cal-A.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Divide Cal-A gene into three parts for independent mutagenesis.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Gene divided into part 1 (N-terminal), part 2 (tunnel region), part 3 (C-terminal).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design mutagenesis strategies for each part.

evidence.quote_or_span: These methods typically suffer from one or more disadvantage including high cost, being experimentally demanding, requiring time consuming steps or, importantly, not providing flexibility in recombining mutations.
The three ‘parts’ of Cal-A were synthesized in a codon-optimized form

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Cal-A gene divided into three parts.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which mutagenesis strategies to apply to each part to explore substrate selectivity.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Targeted NDT saturation mutagenesis at Tyr93, Tyr183, Phe431 and random mutagenesis of the tunnel region will generate functional diversity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Apply NDT saturation mutagenesis to Tyr93, Tyr183, Phe431; error-prone PCR to tunnel region (part 2).
evolution_trajectory.observation: NDT degeneracy chosen for three positions; error-prone PCR for tunnel region.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design assembly method to recombine mutated parts.

evidence.quote_or_span: We chose NDT degeneracy at these three positions
the entire tunnel region (residues 211-350) was targeted for random mutagenesis

Based on further structural analysis (PDB identification code: 2VEO)[26] and proximity to the catalytic triad (within 5 Å from the hydroxyl oxygen of Ser183), residues Tyr93, Tyr183 and Phe431 were selected as potential hot-spots for mutagenesis.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a seamless method to recombine mutated parts into expression vector.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Golden Gate assembly with BsaI and SapI will allow seamless recombination of parts.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design junctions with BsaI and SapI sites for Golden Gate assembly into daughter vector pD441pelB.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Junctions designed with unique BsaI site; two SapI sites for ligation into daughter vector.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Generate libraries using the designed assembly.

evidence.quote_or_span: The junctions between parts were designed with a unique BsaI restriction site while two unique SapI restriction sites were designed to ligate the assembled gene into the daughter vector
When ready to be recombined, for example upon library generation, the parts were reassembled into the complete gene in an Electra daughter vector (pD441pelB, DNA2.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether the assembly strategy can generate diverse libraries.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Golden Gate assembly will yield at least 10^3 transformants per library with various combinations.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Generate ten libraries with combinations of mutated and native parts.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Ten libraries obtained with at least 10^3 transformants each; one library (Tyr93-Phe431) initially failed with circularized plasmid.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Assess library quality and resolve failure.

evidence.quote_or_span: We thus rapidly obtained a variety of complex, mutated libraries, ready to bring forward to screening (Table 1).
In one instance (construction of library Tyr93-Phe431), use of the circularized daughter plasmid afforded no transformants. This issue was resolved by using the commercial, pre-cut daughter plasmid

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Libraries generated with one failure resolved.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether libraries have expected diversity and quality.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Sequencing will confirm expected diversity for NDT libraries and mutational load for random library.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Sequence pooled clones from NDT libraries and 20 clones from random library.
evolution_trajectory.observation: NDT libraries showed expected codon degeneracy; random library had total mutational load 34, 25% wild-type, average 1.65 mutations/clone, max 4.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed to screening of libraries.

evidence.quote_or_span: The quality of each of the degenerate NDT libraries, whether resulting from mutation of one part or assembly of more than one mutated part, was routinely assessed by sequencing pooled clones.
codon degeneracy can be clearly visible in the DNA sequencing electropherogram (Fig 3), confirming the expected distribution of nucleotides instead of the original codon.
The quality of the randomly mutated library was assessed by sequencing 20 clones (Table 2).

The 20 clones carried a total mutational load of 34, with approximately 25% of the clones being wild-type and the majority of clones carrying two mutations (average = 1.65), for a maximum of 4 mutations per clone.
